recap: Created is-python autoload function (extension-only, no shebang) with 6 BATS tests covering .py, other extensions, no extension, symlink, directory, and missing path.
Created python-test-path script: returns already-a-test paths as-is, resolves source files to __tests__/test_{basename}.py, exits non-zero for missing tests or no argument; 5 BATS tests.
Extended python-lint to accept multiple file arguments; loops over each applying ruff format+check+report, exits non-zero if any file has remaining violations; single-file still works.

Added Python branch to git-file-lint (is-python detection, python-lint --fix call, displayPythonLintErrors helper); upgraded zsh-lint call to pass --fix; added 4 new BATS tests including --fix flag assertions for both ZSH and Python.

Added Python branch to git-file-test: is-python detection in file loop, python-test-path call to resolve test path, python-test call if any test paths found; 4 new BATS tests covering pass, no-test, fail, and non-Python-unaffected cases.
Added lint-python and test-python yarn wrappers, registered them in package.json, added **/*.py entry to lintstaged.config.js, and upgraded lint-zsh to pass --fix to zsh-lint.
